narrative:
Today is much different than recent days with record warmth. Temperatures fall through the day and winds are strong and gusty. Rain this morning ends as snow, especially in areas along the TN border, and may accumulate briefly. We have below freezing temperatures overnight, the first time in awhile, but Friday is a dry day with near average temperatures. Saturday is a great day with plenty of sun and milder temperatures. Temperatures remain mild Sunday but light rain, drizzle and fog develop by afternoon and continue overnight. Significantly colder weather returns Monday.

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Wild Spring Swings

Last Updated Thu March 12 at 10:52AM by Wayne Presnell

Today is much different than recent days with record warmth. Temperatures fall through the day and winds are strong and gusty. Rain this morning ends as snow, especially in areas along the TN border, and may accumulate briefly. We have below freezing temperatures overnight, the first time in awhile, but Friday is a dry day with near average temperatures. Saturday is a great day with plenty of sun and milder temperatures. Temperatures remain mild Sunday but light rain, drizzle and fog develop by afternoon and continue overnight. Significantly colder weather returns Monday.

Forecast Discussion

A cold front passed through earlier this morning. Rain behind the front should end by late morning. There is enough cold air moving in that many places will see the precipitation end as snow and it may accumulate briefly, especially in higher elevations and along the TN border.

Temperatures are at their warmest this morning and fall through the day. Winds are strong and gusty from the west and northwest. It's a sudden return to winter from the late spring like days we've had recently.

High pressure builds in overnight allowing skies to clear and temperatures to drop well below freezing, something we have not experienced in awhile. The center of the high shifts to our east by Saturday. Friday is a pleasant day with near average temperatures and Saturday is even better as temperatures are noticeably milder.

Changes begin to occur Sunday. Warm moist air via a southeasterly flow in the low levels begins to move into the area. Temperatures remain mild but clouds and spotty light rain and drizzle develop. Fog likely becomes an issue in the late afternoon and evening.

A strong cold front blasts through Monday morning. Morning rain turns to snow and snow showers as temperatures fall through the day. Winds will be very strong and gusty. Snow showers and flurries continue Monday night. Temperatures fall into the teens for Tuesday and Wednesday morning. Winds slowly ease up by Tuesday night as high pressure settles in overhead. Temperatures are well below average for the first half of next week but begin to warm up on Wednesday.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Monday Message for March 9, 2026

It's March Madness, weather-style, around western North Carolina. We discuss the ups-and-downs of March weather in this week's edition.

Mar 09, 2026
Spring Fever and near-record temperatures

Warm weather is here, at least until the middle of next week. We will be flirting with daily records at a number of locations during this time period.

On Friday, high temperatures will be in record territory for many locations.

On this map, a '1' denotes a forecast high temperature that would surpass a high temperature record for the 6th of March.

Mar 05, 2026
Monday Message for March 2, 2026

It's the first Monday Message for March! We discuss the chilly change for Monday and Tuesday, but the big weather story will be the spring fever that will be breaking out for the second half of the week.
